Using DOMPurify in Vue 3 to Prevent XSS Attacks
In Vue, `v-html` turns an HTML string into actual DOM. That introduces XSS risk — there's no guarantee user input is safe, so we need to sanitise it to prevent malicious scripts from executing.
A Friendly Look at Functional Programming in JavaScript: Currying

Functional Programming has this one really neat idea called Currying. In this post I'll walk through what Currying actually is, how to build it from scratch, and where you might use it in real code. Spoiler: it's not some fancy new syntax — it's just closures doing their thing.
Why You Should Understand Memory Leaks (memory leak)

"Memory" is a word that matters to every developer — how memory gets used directly affects how your program runs. This post walks through what memory leaks are, how garbage collection actually works, and three common patterns in JavaScript that quietly leak memory: forgotten event listeners, runaway timers, and closures that hold onto more than they should.
Hit a weird `useFetch` issue in Nuxt 3 recently — once the API errors out, you can't trigger it again. Here's a note on what's happening.
Prettier Tutorial: You'll End Up Using It Anyway — Why Not Start Now? (Full VSCode Setup)

Prettier is one of those frontend tools you have to know. Even if you're not using it today, you'll end up using it eventually — so why not start now? This post walks through installing Prettier, configuring it, setting it up in VSCode, and how it differs from ESLint.
Why My Blog Wouldn't Update: A Service Workers Stale-Cache Story

Readers kept telling me my blog hadn't been updated in ages — but I was posting regularly. After some digging it turned out to be a Service Workers issue left over from when I'd dropped PWA from my blog. This post walks through how I diagnosed it, how to manually unregister Service Workers in Chrome DevTools, and how to auto-unregister them for visitors so they don't get stuck on a stale cache.
Vue Custom Components Lost Their Highlighting in VSCode — Here's the Fix
Noticed Volar's custom-component highlighting wasn't working right, so I dug into it and found the cause — sharing the fix in case you hit the same thing.
Mac: Auto-Switch Node.js Version via nvm When Opening a Project

As a Node.js dev, switching Node.js versions comes up all the time. Each project uses a different version, and it's super easy to forget to switch. So here's a note on auto-switching with nvm whenever you open a project on Mac.

Hit a gnarly `useFetch` gotcha in Nuxt 3 recently — and apparently lots of folks have run into the same thing. So here's a note on what's happening and how to fix it.
What Is Encryption? Why Do We Encrypt? A Beginner's Take

Something came up recently that made me want to write a beginner-level post on encryption. This one covers what encryption actually is, why we use it, the difference between symmetric and asymmetric encryption (with examples like AES, RSA, and how GitHub SSH works), and a section on Base64 — which a lot of people mistake for encryption but is actually just encoding.
